name: Saccharomyces boulardii
summary: Decision-ready summary: Saccharomyces boulardii is best framed for antibiotic-associated diarrhea risk; traveler’s diarrhea context; gut resilience; safety screen: central venous catheter; severe immunocompromise; critical illness.
description: Saccharomyces boulardii supports antibiotic-associated diarrhea management by reinforcing gut barrier and immune signaling while limiting pathogen effects.
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated for most users.

claimMap.claim: v10.4: Meta-analysis found S. boulardii significantly reduced antibiotic-associated diarrhea risk in adults.
claimMap.notes: v10.4: Meta-analysis found S. boulardii significantly reduced antibiotic-associated diarrhea risk in adults.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: Common clinical range: 250–1000 mg/day or product-specific CFU dosing

claimMap.claim: v9.2: Systematic review/meta-analysis found significant efficacy for prevention of antibiotic-associated diarrhea.
claimMap.notes: v9.2: Systematic review/meta-analysis found significant efficacy for prevention of antibiotic-associated diarrhea.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: Common clinical range: 250–1000 mg/day or strain/product-specific CFU dosing
